ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for burrito as a prepared dish; not clinically coded at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Little Guy Burrito weighs approximately 200 g, representing a single wrapped serving typically consumed as a lunch or dinner meal.

nutrient_source.title: Little Guy Burrito — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Little Guy Burrito (cr_id G00010676). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins/minerals not reported in the HPB record are set to -1 (data not available).

gi_source.title: Burrito with chicken, rice, beans, cheese and salsa — analogous estimate based on flour tortilla-based mixed dish GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 'Little Guy Burrito' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 52 based on the closest analogous entry for a flour tortilla-based mixed dish (chicken burrito with rice, beans, and cheese) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The flour tortilla contributes the dominant glycaemic load; beans and protein moderate the overall GI. GL per serving calculated as (52 × 20.06 g carbohydrate per 100g × 200g serving) / 100 = 20.9 g. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 470 mg/100g with low potassium offset (126 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: At 470 mg sodium per 100g and only 126 mg potassium per 100g, a 200g serving delivers approximately 940 mg sodium — nearly 41% of the 2300 mg daily limit — making this dish unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals managing hypertension; request reduced cheese and sour cream, ask for no added salt in the filling, and consider salsa on the side to limit further sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 20.06 g/100g with low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100g) from refined flour tortilla
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined flour tortilla contributes a moderate-to-high glycaemic load (estimated GL 20.9 per 200g serving) with only 1.4 g dietary fibre per 100g to slow glucose absorption; individuals with type 2 diabetes should request a whole-wheat tortilla, reduce rice portion, and increase beans to improve the fibre-to-carbohydrate ratio and blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at 4.8 g/100g and cholesterol 212 mg/100g from cheese, sour cream, and chicken
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: With an estimated 4.8 g saturated fat and 212 mg cholesterol per 100g, a 200g serving provides approximately 9.6 g saturated fat (approaching the 10g daily limit for cardiovascular risk management) and 424 mg dietary cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request reduced cheese and sour cream, and choose grilled chicken over fried to lower saturated fat and cholesterol intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat near 5 g/100g; nightshade ingredients (tomatoes, salsa); low anti-inflammatory fibre
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Cheese and sour cream contribute saturated fat near the 5 g/100g threshold, and tomatoes and salsa are nightshade foods that some individuals with rheumatoid arthritis report as joint-flare triggers; request chicken filling over beef to reduce arachidonic acid load, reduce cheese and sour cream, and monitor personal tolerance to nightshade ingredients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Little Guy Burrito is a Mexican-American wrapped dish sold in Singapore and listed in the HPB Foodo nutrient database (cr_id G00010676). It consists of a flour tortilla wrapped around a filling of chicken, rice, beans, cheese, sour cream, salsa, and tomatoes. The tortilla is typically soft and lightly griddled, yielding a warm, slightly crisp exterior with a moist, savoury interior. The flavour profile is mildly spiced with a creamy, tangy note from the sour cream and cheese. Per 100g, the burrito provides 249 kcal, 10.91g protein, 13.71g fat (estimated 4.8g saturated), 20.06g carbohydrate, 1.75g sugar, 1.4g dietary fibre, 470mg sodium, 126mg potassium, 148mg calcium, 16mg magnesium, 2.28mg iron, and 212mg cholesterol. The relatively high sodium content (470mg/100g) and moderate saturated fat make it a food to consume in moderation for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The low dietary fibre from the refined flour tortilla and the moderate carbohydrate load are relevant considerations for individuals with type 2 diabetes. A 200g serving is a typical single-meal portion. The dish is suitable for lunch or dinner and is commonly available at casual dining and fast-casual Mexican-American restaurants in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Little Guy Burrito (cr_id G00010676, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from typical burrito composition as the HPB record does not report it separately; this field is noted as an estimate. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for a comparable flour tortilla-based mixed dish. All other HPB-reported fields are accepted at high confidence.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (4.8 g/100g) is estimated from typical chicken burrito composition (cheese, sour cream, chicken) as the HPB source does not report saturated fat separately; this value should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific burrito preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact product as of 2026. 3. The input record incorrectly assigned primary_cuisine as 'Japanese'; this has been corrected to 'Mexican-American' based on the dish identity. 4. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for a flour tortilla-based chicken burrito; a direct GI measurement for this specific product is not available. GL per 200g serving = (52 × 20.06 × 200) / 100 / 100 = 20.9. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the HPB record does not specify halal certification.
